general_description: Tabular gradient-boosted classifier (XGBoost v2.0) trained on the UCI Statlog (German Credit) dataset to score retail-loan creditworthiness. Provider: Acme Credit AI Ltd. Person responsible: Head of Model Risk. Intended to support, not replace, human credit officers.
design_process: Stratified 80/20 train/test split; 5-fold CV with grid-search hyper-parameter tuning; calibration via isotonic regression; threshold tuned to maximise expected utility under the asymmetric cost matrix recommended in the UCI German Credit metadata.
training_data_description: UCI Statlog (German Credit Data) - 1000 instances, 20 attributes (7 numerical, 13 categorical), binary creditworthy target (1 = good risk, 0 = bad risk). Class prior 70/30 good:bad. No sensitive demographic features beyond age (used per Art. 10 derogation only for bias monitoring).
data_governance_measures: Data minimisation per Art. 10 §2(b); access restricted to model-development team; provenance documented; quarterly re-collection from the same source; no further enrichment from external broker data.
monitoring_measures: Monthly performance review on a held-out production sample; drift detection on the input feature distribution via Population Stability Index; alerting threshold PSI > 0.2.
logging_capabilities: Per-prediction log entry recording timestamp, hashed feature vector, model version, returned score, and decision threshold band per Art. 12 §1.
lifecycle_change_log: v1.0.0 initial release on UCI German Credit
